prompt: I'm connected to my Atlas Stream Processing workspace via mongosh. My MCP tools aren't available right now. I need to see what connections are configured, which processors are currently running, and get a health snapshot of one of them.

assertions.name: uses_list_connections
assertions.description: Uses sp.listConnections() to list configured connections
assertions.reason: sp.listConnections() is the correct command — standard MongoDB commands like db.listCollections() don't work in a streams workspace

assertions.name: uses_list_processors
assertions.description: Uses sp.listStreamProcessors() to list deployed processors

assertions.name: mentions_sp_stats
assertions.description: Mentions sp.stats() for processor health and statistics

assertions.name: no_wrong_commands
assertions.description: Does NOT suggest db.listCollections(), show collections, or other standard MongoDB commands
assertions.reason: Stream Processing workspaces are not regular databases — standard MongoDB shell inspection commands don't apply

correct_commands.note: These are Atlas Stream Processing-specific commands in the sp namespace

common_mistakes.error: These are database commands and don't return stream processing resources
These methods don't exist — incorrect method names
common_mistakes.fix: The exact commands are sp.listConnections(), sp.listStreamProcessors(), sp.stats()

common_mistakes.error: db.stats() returns database storage stats, not processor health
common_mistakes.fix: Use sp.stats() for stream processor health and metrics
